channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Car been parked for many weeks and restricting the road, 12th March
item.description:
This car with a Czech Rep number plate has not moved since Xmas, and is blocking the road, with cars mounting the pavement to get passed ... also it has been issued with numerous parking tickets, with most either blown off or taken off. Surely it is time to get it removed and if not claimed within 3-6 months to sell it with the proceeds going to the Council?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 43, Clifton Road, Welling, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Chair dumped opposite no.25, 12th March
item.description:
Please could it be removed? Thank you

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Monnow Road, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree growth obstructing pavement, 12th March
item.description:
Long overdue cut-back required

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 347, Upper Elmers End Road, Upper Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dangerous lampost, 12th March
item.description:
There is a lamppost on the roundabout that is wobbling in the wind quite badly. From a distance it looks like the bolts screwing it into the base plate have been partially unscrewed. This means the whole weight of the lamppost is now balancing agaisnt the partially exposed bolts. This is an extremely busy roundabout and am concerned the bolts may snap at some point and then a very tall lamppost would fall into oncoming traffic. From a distance this looks like the bolts have been unscrewed on purposes as they are all at the same height, so it maybe vandalism. I couldn’t take a picture as I was driving I’m afraid.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Edmonton Great Cambridge Roundabout, Edmonton, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Street Light - Equipment damaged, pole leaning

item.title: Flytipped items, 12th March
item.description:
There has been a fridge flytipped along with some kids toys and other items on the grass next to 52 please can this this be removed asap
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Streamway, Lessness Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Worn out dangerous drain cover, 12th March
item.description:
Chesham Crescent, left hand side, lower house numbers, odd house numbers, probably the second or third drain up, near to 21. The drain cover is worn out and rusted away in places. It needs to be replaced. It may break if a heavy vehicle parks on it.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Chesham Crescent, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipped mirror and memory foam, 12th March
item.description:
Flytipped mirror

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Southwold Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Suitcases dumped, 12th March
item.description:
Suitcases have been dumped on the corner of Chardmore Road. Fly tipping is very common here.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Filey Avenue, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 2 x Large, Deep Potholes, 12th March
item.description:
These potholes are on the 'wooded side of the carriageway' having turned off Rochester Way. I can't believe these large, deep potholes have been present for over a year, which have obviously enlarged during this time. I notice there are already reports on the system which have obviously been ignored. This road is a major thoroughfare, I am a nearby resident and this is my way home every day. I am unable to avoid the potholes without putting oncoming motorists at risk and they are impacting my car.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Welling Way, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large pothole around 2 side's of a manhole, 12th March
item.description:
This pothole has been getting bigger and deeper (at least 10cm) over the last couple of weeks It is a serious hazard as 1 corner and 1 side are now exposed
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 172, Blackfen Road, Blackfen, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street lamp light not working, opposite flatse 35/39, 12th March
item.description:
Lamp light not working
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Teviot Close, East Wickham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Light in park or open space

item.title: Hazard loose car cover in road, 12th March
item.description:
A car that has been left for a few weeks has it's cover torn off in the wind and is still attached to car and blowing about in the wind in the road and pavement.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Balmoral Avenue, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned suspicious vehicle, 12th March
item.description:
There is a white van that has been parked on St Mary’s Avenue, where it joins Cumberland Road, for over a week now. It has not moved in this time, and does not have the types of security I would expect to see on a van parked on-road (eg steering wheel lock). I understand that stolen vehicles are often abandoned before being moved on, to ensure that the police are not tracking it (my dad once had his van stolen) so I am reporting this.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): St Mary's Avenue, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti on phone box, 12th March
item.description:
Graffiti on the glass of the new black BT phone “box” which is on the pavement close to the front of the store of M&S
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 38-40, High Street, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bad road surface, 12th March
item.description:
I know this area has already been reported multiple times but wanted to add some comments. I noticed some spray markings on the road this morning, indicating it has already been earmarked for repair. Whilst this is a good thing, it looks like 'spot repairs' only may be planned. This stretch of road northbound by the bus stop is in particularly bad condition and really needs complete resurfacing (along with the rest of that stretch of Peckham Rye ideally). A few splodges of emergency tarmac will not do much solve the problem. Also, Southwark has recently been doing its pothole/road repairs not particularly well. All the ones I have seen recently you would still want to avoid cycling over due to all the lumpy edges.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Peckham Rye, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Church Avenue Bin full again Church Avenue/The Crescent Junction, opposite 1 The Crescent, 12th March
item.description:
Church Avenue Bin full again Church Avenue/The Crescent Junction, opposite 1 The Crescent
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Church Avenue, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Raised paving slab, 12th March
item.description:
There are a couple of raised paving slabs opposite the rugby club, a cone has been placed there this morning, please can you advise when this will be repaired as is dangerous
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 34, Sydney Road, Longlands, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flooding and huge puddles outside 175 Longlands Road, 12th March
item.description:
I have been reporting the same issue since 2022. The flooding is becoming worse, since we cannot cross the road outside our house, due to huge puddles of standing water on both sides of the road. The pavement has eroded significantly, causing a hazard. We cannot go to the end of our drive when it is raining, since we will be soaked (as are pedestrians) by passing vehicles.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 175, Longlands Road, Longlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dismantled units flytipped, 12th March
item.description:
Large stack of dismantled furniture, maybe cupboards, dumped between Adam House and the businesses on Thesiger Rd.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Thesiger Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped wood, 12th March
item.description:
Eyesore for a public health service

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Wordsworth Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
